sanskrit: न मे पार्थास्ति कर्तव्यं त्रिषु लोकेषु किञ्चन । नानवाप्तमवाप्तव्यं वर्त एव च कर्मणि ॥
hindiTranslation: हे पार्थ मुझे तीनों लोकोंमें न तो कुछ कर्तव्य है और न कोई प्राप्त करनेयोग्य वस्तु अप्राप्त है, फिर भी मैं कर्तव्यकर्ममें ही लगा रहता हूँ ॥
englishTranslation: O Partha, there is nothing in the three worlds that I must do, and nothing I have not yet gained that must be gained. Yet I remain engaged in action.
essence: Even the fulfilled keep working, because action need not come from lack.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Krishna says he has no unfinished duty and nothing left to gain, yet he still stays active.
simpleInsight: Real freedom is not doing less; it is acting without inner lack.
detailedMeaning: On Kurukshetra, Arjuna is frozen before battle. Krishna explains why he himself still acts: not from need, but to guide the world. This verse turns Krishna into the model for action without craving.
modernRelevance: You’ve already won the argument in your head, but you still show up for the hard conversation. You do it not to get something, but because the action itself matters.
translationLiteral: न (not) / मे (for me) / पार्थ (O Partha) / अस्ति (there is) / कर्तव्यम् (to be done / duty) / त्रिषु (in the three) / लोकेषु (worlds) / किञ्चन (anything) / न (not) / अनवाप्तम् (not attained) / अवाप्तव्यम् (to be attained) / वर्ते (I remain / I engage) / एव (indeed) / च (and) / कर्मणि (in action)
